class.name: GDKSocial
class.brief_description: Social graph service accessed via GDK.social.
class.description: Wraps the Xbox Social Manager and reputation feedback APIs. Start tracking a user's social graph with [method start_social_graph], create filtered groups with [method create_social_group], retrieve user data with [method get_group_users], and submit feedback with [method submit_reputation_feedback_async].

method.name: start_social_graph
method.description: Starts tracking the social graph for the given local user. Must be called before creating social groups.

method.name: stop_social_graph
method.description: Stops tracking the social graph for the given local user.

method.name: get_friends_async
method.description: Asynchronously retrieves the friends list for the given local user. Await the returned completion [Signal] to receive a [GDKResult] whose [code]data[/code] contains the loaded [GDKSocialGroup].

method.name: create_social_group
method.description: Creates a social group for the given user with an optional filter. If no filter is provided, a default filter is used. Returns a [GDKResult]; on success the [code]data[/code] field contains the created [GDKSocialGroup]. On failure the result describes the error and a [code]runtime_error[/code] signal is emitted.

method.name: create_social_group_from_xuids
method.description: Creates a social group that tracks the specified XUIDs. Returns a [GDKResult]; on success the [code]data[/code] field contains the created [GDKSocialGroup]. On failure the result describes the error and a [code]runtime_error[/code] signal is emitted.

method.name: destroy_social_group
method.description: Destroys the given social group and stops tracking its users.

method.name: get_group_users
method.description: Returns a [GDKResult] whose [code]data[/code] field contains an [Array] of [GDKSocialUser] objects for the users tracked by the given group. On native failure the result describes the error and a [code]runtime_error[/code] signal is emitted.

method.name: submit_reputation_feedback_async
method.description: Submits one Xbox Services reputation feedback item for [param target_xuid]. Supported [param feedback_type] values are [code]fair_play_kills_teammates[/code], [code]fair_play_cheater[/code], [code]fair_play_tampering[/code], [code]fair_play_quitter[/code], [code]fair_play_kicked[/code], [code]communications_inappropriate_video[/code], [code]communications_abusive_voice[/code], [code]inappropriate_user_generated_content[/code], [code]positive_skilled_player[/code], [code]positive_helpful_player[/code], [code]positive_high_quality_user_generated_content[/code], [code]comms_phishing[/code], [code]comms_picture_message[/code], [code]comms_spam[/code], [code]comms_text_message[/code], [code]comms_voice_message[/code], [code]fair_play_console_ban_request[/code], [code]fair_play_idler[/code], [code]fair_play_user_ban_request[/code], [code]user_content_gamerpic[/code], [code]user_content_personal_info[/code], [code]fair_play_unsporting[/code], and [code]fair_play_leaderboard_cheater[/code]. Unknown feedback types fail with [code]invalid_feedback_type[/code]. Await the returned completion [Signal] directly.

method.name: submit_batch_reputation_feedback_async
method.description: Submits reputation feedback for multiple users. Each item is a [Dictionary] with [code]target_xuid[/code] and [code]feedback_type[/code], plus optional [code]reason[/code] and [code]evidence_id[/code]. Empty arrays fail with [code]invalid_feedback_items[/code]; non-dictionary items or dictionaries missing [code]target_xuid[/code]/[code]feedback_type[/code] fail with [code]invalid_feedback_item[/code]; unknown feedback types fail with [code]invalid_feedback_type[/code].

signal.name: social_graph_changed
signal.description: Emitted when the social graph for a local user has changed.

signal.name: social_group_updated
signal.description: Emitted when a social group's tracked user list has been updated.

signal.name: social_user_changed
signal.description: Emitted when a tracked social user's data has changed.

signal.name: runtime_error
signal.description: Emitted when an unsolicited social subsystem error occurs (for example a failure inside [code]XblSocialManagerDoWork[/code], a per-event failure delivered by Social Manager, or a native failure raised while creating a group or reading group users). Caller-driven failures are also returned as the [GDKResult] from [method create_social_group], [method create_social_group_from_xuids], and [method get_group_users].
